content.text:
تسک‌های باقی‌مانده انجام شد. خلاصه:

## انجام‌شده

### ۱. مهاجرت `/book` به `bookingSlice`
- همه stateهای ویزارد رزرو (مرحله، سرویس، پرسنل، تاریخ، تأیید و…) از `useState` به Redux منتقل شد
- نام کاربر از `authSlice` خوانده می‌شود (بعد از login)

### ۲. مهاجرت `/login` به `authSlice`
- بعد از تأیید OTP، `setCredentials` + `saveToken` فراخوانی می‌شود
- ذخیره نام پروفایل با `updateUserName` در Redux

### ۳. اتصال `/profile` به `authSlice`
- نام و شماره از Redux (با fallback به mock برای حالت demo)
- ویرایش نام → `updateUserName`

### ۴. تأیید کیفیت
- `npm run typecheck` — بدون خطا

---

## وضعیت کلی پروژه

| بخش | وضعیت |
|-----|--------|
| RTK shell + hooks | آماده |
| codebase-memory-mcp | ایندکس شده |
| Cursor rules (۳ فایل) | فعال |
| AGENTS.md | آماده |
| Redux store + slices | آماده |
| مهاجرت صفحات customer | انجام شد |

---

## کارهای اختیاری بعدی

1. **Restart Cursor** — اگر هنوز نکردید، برای بارگذاری MCP و rules
2. **Commit** — شامل `.codebase-memory/graph.db.zst` و تغییرات Redux
3. **Postman MCP** — تنظیم `PMAK-...` اگر API تست می‌خواهید
4. **Hydration auth** — بازیابی session از `localStorage` در `StoreProvider` (برای refresh صفحه)

اگر بخواهید، می‌توانم hydration auth یا commit را هم انجام دهم.

content.text:
هر دو کار انجام شد.

## ۱. Auth Hydration

Session بعد از refresh صفحه از `localStorage` بازیابی می‌شود:

- **`src/lib/auth.ts`** — توابع `loadSession`، `saveSession`، `clearSession` (کلیدهای `auth_token` + `auth_user`)
- **`src/providers/StoreProvider.tsx`** — در mount، session را load می‌کند و `setCredentials` می‌زند؛ با subscribe هر تغییر auth را خودکار persist می‌کند
- **`login/page.tsx`** — دیگر مستقیم `saveToken` نمی‌زند؛ persist از طریق store listener انجام می‌شود

**تست:** login کنید → refresh → نام کاربر در `/book` و `/profile` باید بماند.

---

## ۲. Commit

```
e338c1c — Add Cursor agent tooling, Redux store, and auth session persistence.
```

**۲۲ فایل** شامل:
- `.codebase-memory/graph.db.zst`
- `.cursor/` (rules, hooks, mcp)
- `AGENTS.md`
- Redux store + migrations
- Auth hydration

Branch یک commit جلوتر از `origin/master` است. اگر بخواهید push کنم بگویید.

---

**نکته:** کلید Postman در `.cursor/mcp.json` به `${POSTMAN_API_KEY}` تغییر کرد تا secret داخل repo نباشد. متغیر env را در Cursor تنظیم کنید.

input.description: Create RTK.md and link from AGENTS.md

input.contents:
---
description: MANDATORY — prefix every shell command with rtk (Rust Token Killer). Applies to ALL prompts and agents in this project.
alwaysApply: true
---

# RTK — اجباری در همه پرامپت‌ها (Mandatory)

> **CRITICAL / BLOCKING:** Before running ANY shell command in this project, prefix it with `rtk`.
> This rule applies to **every prompt**, **every agent turn**, and **every subagent** — no exceptions except active debugging.

RTK (Rust Token Killer) = `C:\Users\kasha\.local\bin\rtk.exe` — compresses shell output 60–99% with zero behavior change.
Unsupported commands pass through unchanged — **always safe to prefix**.

**Dual enforcement:**
1. This rule (`alwaysApply: true`) — agent MUST write `rtk` in every shell command
2. `.cursor/hooks.json` — `preToolUse` auto-rewrites via `rtk-rewrite.ps1` + `rtk hook cursor`

Do **not** rely on hooks alone — always prefix `rtk` yourself.

See also: `RTK.md`, `AGENTS.md` §8.

---

## Universal Rule

```
❌ git status
✅ rtk git status

❌ npm run build
✅ rtk npm run build

❌ cmd1 && cmd2
✅ rtk cmd1 && rtk cmd2
```

In PowerShell chains, prefix **each** segment:
```powershell
rtk git add . ; rtk git commit -m "msg"
```

---

## Beauty Saloon Frontend — Next.js Commands

This is a **Next.js 16 / React 19 / TypeScript** app. Prefer these RTK-wrapped commands:

| Task | Command |
|------|---------|
| Dev server | `rtk npm run dev` |
| Production build | `rtk npm run build` |
| Type check | `rtk npm run typecheck` |
| Lint | `rtk npm run lint` |
| Install package | `rtk npm install <pkg>` |
| Next.js CLI | `rtk npx next <cmd>` |

For npm scripts without a native RTK filter, use `rtk err npm run <script>` or `rtk summary npm run build`.

---

## Standard Commands (All Projects)

```bash
# Git (59–80% savings)
rtk git status          rtk git diff            rtk git log

# Files & Search — LAST RESORT only (codebase-memory MCP must be tried first)
rtk grep <pattern>      rtk find <pattern>
rtk read <file>         rtk ls <path>           rtk diff <file>

# Analysis (70–90% savings)
rtk err <cmd>           rtk log <file>          rtk json <file>
rtk summary <cmd>       rtk deps                rtk env

# GitHub (26–87% savings)
rtk gh pr view <n>      rtk gh run list         rtk gh issue list
```

---

## Token Optimization Stack (Use Both)

| Layer | Tool | Saves tokens on |
|-------|------|-----------------|
| Code exploration | `codebase-memory-mcp` (`search_graph`, `search_code`, `get_code_snippet`) | Finding symbols, call chains, architecture |
| Shell output | `rtk` | Git, build, test — and **only as last-resort** `rtk grep` |

**NEVER** use Cursor `Grep`/`Glob`/broad `Read` for code search — use codebase-memory MCP first (see `codebase-memory.mdc`).
**NEVER** run raw shell commands when RTK can filter the output.

---

## Exceptions (Rare)

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Active debugging / need full verbose output | Raw command, no `rtk` prefix |
| Interactive command (`git rebase -i`, etc.) | Not supported — avoid interactive commands |
| Unknown if RTK supports command | Prefix anyway — passes through if unsupported |

---

## Anti-Patterns

```
❌ Running shell without rtk prefix (even for "small" commands)
❌ Using Cursor Grep/Glob before codebase-memory search_graph/search_code
❌ Running 10+ file reads via shell without rtk
❌ npm run build without rtk npm (dumps thousands of lines)
❌ git log without rtk (wastes tokens on large repos)
❌ Skipping rtk because "output is small" — habit matters, prefix always
```

input.contents:
# RTK - Rust Token Killer

**Usage**: Token-optimized CLI proxy (60–90% savings on shell output)

## Rule (Mandatory)

**Every shell command MUST be prefixed with `rtk`.** Applies to all agents and prompts in this project.

```bash
rtk git status
rtk npm run dev
rtk npm run build
rtk npm run typecheck
rtk npm run lint
rtk npm install <pkg>
```

PowerShell chains — prefix each segment:
```powershell
rtk git add . ; rtk git commit -m "msg"
```

## Meta Commands

```bash
rtk gain              # Token savings analytics
rtk gain --history    # Recent command savings history
rtk proxy <cmd>       # Run raw command without filtering (debug only)
rtk --version         # Verify install (expect rtk 0.x)
```

## Verification

```bash
where rtk             # → C:\Users\kasha\.local\bin\rtk.exe
rtk gain
```

## Project Integration

- **Cursor rule:** `.cursor/rules/rtk.mdc` (`alwaysApply: true`)
- **Hooks:** `.cursor/hooks.json` → auto-rewrite shell via `rtk-rewrite.ps1` + `rtk hook cursor`
- **Agent manual:** `AGENTS.md` §8

> Note: "RTK" = **Rust Token Killer** (`rtk.exe`), not Redux Toolkit.

content.text:
RTK (Rust Token Killer) نصب و اجبار شد.

## وضعیت نصب

| مورد | وضعیت |
|------|--------|
| **Binary** | `C:\Users\kasha\.local\bin\rtk.exe` — نسخه **0.43.0** |
| **Global hook** | `rtk init -g --agent cursor --auto-patch` |
| **Project hook** | `.cursor/hooks.json` + `.cursor/hooks/rtk-rewrite.ps1` |

---

## اجبار در Cursor (همه پرامپت‌ها)

| فایل | نقش |
|------|-----|
| `.cursor/rules/rtk.mdc` | `alwaysApply: true` — prefix اجباری `rtk` روی هر shell |
| `.cursor/hooks.json` | auto-rewrite با `rtk-rewrite.ps1` + `rtk hook cursor` |
| `RTK.md` | راهنمای کوتاه RTK |
| `AGENTS.md` | `@RTK.md` در ابتدای فایل |

---

## نحوه استفاده

```powershell
rtk git status
rtk npm run dev
rtk npm run typecheck
rtk npm run build
```

در PowerShell هر segment را جدا prefix کنید:
```powershell
rtk git add . ; rtk git commit -m "msg"
```

---

## یک کار دستی

**Cursor را یک‌بار reload/restart کنید** تا hooks و rules جدید بارگذاری شوند.

---

تغییرات هنوز commit نشده‌اند. اگر بخواهید commit کنم بگویید.

content.text:
هر دو مشکل برطرف شد.

## ۱. ورود به پنل ادمین

**قبل:** دکمه «ورود به پنل مدیریت» به `/login` می‌رفت، همه کاربران `customer` می‌شدند و `/admin` بدون احراز هویت باز بود.

**الان:**
- دکمه Home → `/login?redirect=/admin/dashboard`
- OTP با `verifyOtpCode` و نقش واقعی کاربر (از mock data)
- **AdminAuthGuard** — فقط `admin` و `staff` به `/admin/*` دسترسی دارند
- مشتری عادی اگر بخواهد وارد admin شود، پیام خطا می‌گیرد

**ورود دمو مدیر:**
```
شماره: 09120000000
OTP:   هر ۵ رقم (مثلاً 12345) — فقط 00000 رد می‌شود
```

---

## ۲. یکپارچه‌سازی پنل (Single Source of Truth)

**قبل:** هر صفحه admin mock data جداگانه داشت — تغییر در dashboard روی appointments تأثیر نمی‌گذاشت.

**الان:** `adminSlice` در Redux همه داده‌ها را یکجا نگه می‌دارد:

| بخش | اتصال |
|-----|--------|
| داشبورد | stats + نوبت‌های امروز + فعالیت‌ها |
| نوبت‌ها | همان لیست نوبت‌ها + فیلتر/تقویم |
| مشتریان | CRUD مشترک |
| پرسنل | CRUD مشترک |
| سرویس‌ها | CRUD مشترک |
| نوبت جدید (modal) | به store اضافه می‌شود → در dashboard و appointments دیده می‌شود |

**مثال:** تأیید نوبت در dashboard → وضعیت در صفحه نوبت‌ها هم عوض می‌شود. ثبت نوبت جدید از header → در همه بخش‌ها ظاهر می‌شود.

---

## تست سریع

1. Home → «ورود به پنل مدیریت»
2. `09120000000` + OTP → داشبورد
3. یک نوبت را تأیید کنید → بروید به «نوبت‌ها» — همان وضعیت را ببینید
4. «نوبت جدید» بزنید → در dashboard و appointments ظاهر شود
5. خروج از sidebar → بدون login مستقیم `/admin` باز نشود

[REDACTED]

<timestamp>Friday, Jul 3, 2026, 10:52 PM (UTC+3:30)</timestamp>
<user_query>
خب توی مرحله بعدی میخوام کرسر رو اجبار کنی که موقع جست و جو تا جایی که امکان داره از دستور های codebase-memory-mcp به جای grep و global استفاده کنه مگه اینکه مجبور باشه به استفاده از این دو تا دستور . و کرسر رو مجبور کن دستور هایی رو که میشه با از codebase-memory-mcp استفاده کرد  به جای دستورات پاورشل اولویت اول رو به codebase-memory بده
</user_query>

قوانین Cursor را تقویت می‌کنم تا codebase-memory-mcp بر Grep، Glob و دستورات shell جستجو اولویت داشته باشد.

input.contents:
---
description: MANDATORY — codebase-memory-mcp FIRST for all code search. Grep/Glob/Shell only as last resort.
alwaysApply: true
---

# Codebase Intelligence — Beauty Saloon Frontend (Mandatory)

> **CRITICAL / BLOCKING:** For **any** code search, exploration, or discovery task, you MUST use **codebase-memory-mcp** tools **before** Cursor `Grep`, `Glob`, broad `Read`, or **any PowerShell/shell search command**.
> This rule applies to **every prompt**, **every agent turn**, and **every subagent**.

This project is indexed by **codebase-memory-mcp** (MCP server: `codebase-memory-mcp`).
The knowledge graph lives in `.codebase-memory/` (~560 nodes).
**You MUST query the graph before exploring or changing code.** Do not guess file locations.

---

## Priority Stack (strict order — never skip)

| Priority | Tool layer | When |
|----------|------------|------|
| **1** | **codebase-memory-mcp** | Code search, structure, symbols, call chains, reading source |
| **2** | Cursor `Read` | **One known file** after MCP identified the path (for editing) |
| **3** | Cursor `Grep` / `Glob` | Only after MCP returned zero useful results — **state why** |
| **4** | Shell (`rtk grep`, `rtk find`, `rtk ls`, `rtk read`) | Only if MCP **and** Grep/Glob both failed — **state why** |

**Shell is for build/git/test — NOT for finding code.** Never reach for PowerShell when an MCP tool can answer.

---

## MCP Replaces Shell Search (mandatory routing)

| ❌ Do NOT use (Cursor or Shell) | ✅ Use MCP instead |
|--------------------------------|-------------------|
| `Grep` / `rg` / `rtk grep` for symbols, functions, components | `search_graph` with `name_pattern` |
| `Grep` / `rtk grep` for error messages, string literals | `search_code` |
| `Glob` / `rtk find` / `Get-ChildItem` for file patterns | `search_graph` with `file_pattern` |
| `Read` 3+ files to "explore" | `get_architecture` → `search_graph` → `get_code_snippet` |
| `rtk ls` / `rtk tree` to understand structure | `get_architecture` |
| `cat` / `type` / `rtk read` to inspect a function | `get_code_snippet` |
| Manual caller hunting | `trace_path` |
| "What did my edit break?" | `detect_changes` |
| Conceptual / fuzzy search | `search_graph` with `semantic_query` |

---

## Project Context (Next.js App Router)

| Area | Path | Role |
|------|------|------|
| **App routes** | `src/app/` | Next.js App Router pages and layouts |
| **Customer** | `src/app/(customer)/` | Customer-facing booking and profile |
| **Auth** | `src/app/(auth)/` | Login / OTP authentication |
| **Admin** | `src/app/admin/` | Admin dashboard, appointments, staff, services, customers |
| **Components** | `src/components/` | UI components by domain (admin, booking, auth, landing, shared) |
| **Data layer** | `src/lib/data/` | Types, mock data, `dataService.ts` |
| **Providers** | `src/providers/` | React context providers (Theme, Redux Store) |
| **Redux store** | `src/store/` | Redux Toolkit slices (`auth`, `booking`, `admin`), hooks |
| **Utilities** | `src/lib/` | Auth helpers, `cn()`, shared utilities |

**Route group conventions:**
- `(customer)/` — public customer flows (`/book`, `/profile`)
- `(auth)/` — authentication (`/login`)
- `admin/` — admin panel with shared `admin/layout.tsx` sidebar

When searching, **scope by domain** (`admin/**` vs `booking/**` vs `(customer)/**`) unless the task spans both.

---

## Universal Rule — Before ANY Code Work

1. Call **`get_architecture`** to understand structure, entry points, routes, and hotspots.
2. Call **`search_graph`** to find related symbols, files, and dependencies.
3. Call **`search_code`** to check existing implementations before creating new ones.
4. Call **`trace_path`** when modifying a function — find all callers and affected files.

**Never assume file locations — always query the graph first.**
**Never create a new page, component, slice, or service without first searching for existing ones.**

Prefer **`get_code_snippet`** over `Read`. Never use `Grep`/`Glob`/shell search when MCP can answer.

---

## Pre-Tool Checklist (run mentally before Grep/Glob/Shell)

Before calling `Grep`, `Glob`, `Read` (exploration), or any shell search command:

1. ☐ Did I call `get_architecture` or `search_graph`?
2. ☐ Did I try `search_code` for text/literals?
3. ☐ Did I try `semantic_query` for conceptual search?
4. ☐ Do I only need one symbol? → `get_code_snippet`
5. ☐ Did **all** MCP tools return zero relevant results?
6. ☐ If using Grep/Glob/Shell fallback — will I **state why** MCP was insufficient?

If steps 1–4 are not done, **stop and use MCP first.**

---

## Workflow: Bug Fix

1. `search_code` — search for the error message, component name, or function.
2. `trace_path` — trace inbound callers to find root cause.
3. `detect_changes` — after fixing, verify blast radius of your edit.
4. Only then implement the fix.

**Frontend-specific patterns to search:**
- Pages: `page.tsx` in `src/app/**`
- Client components: `"use client"` components in `src/components/**`
- Data access: functions in `src/lib/data/dataService.ts`
- State: slices in `src/store/slices/**`

---

## Workflow: New Feature

1. `get_architecture` — confirm where the feature belongs (admin vs customer, which route group).
2. `search_graph` — find similar existing features (e.g. another admin table + modal pattern).
3. `search_graph` with `semantic_query` — find conceptually related code when names differ.
4. Mirror existing patterns: page → client component → dataService / Redux slice.
5. `trace_path` — verify integration points before wiring new code.

---

## Workflow: Refactor / Rename

1. `trace_path` with `direction: "both"` — map full call chain.
2. `detect_changes` — classify risk before and after edits.
3. Check both **admin** and **customer** areas if the symbol is used across domains.

---

## Useful `search_graph` Filters for This Repo

```
name_pattern: ".*Modal"         label: "Function"   file_pattern: "src/components/admin/**"
name_pattern: ".*Table"         label: "Function"   file_pattern: "src/components/admin/**"
name_pattern: ".*Selector"      label: "Function"   file_pattern: "src/components/booking/**"
name_pattern: ".*Slice"         label: "Class"      file_pattern: "src/store/**"
name_pattern: ".*Guard"         label: "Function"   file_pattern: "src/components/**"
name_pattern: "get.*"            label: "Function"   file_pattern: "src/lib/data/**"
semantic_query: ["booking flow", "otp auth", "admin dashboard", "redux admin state"]
```

---

## Tool Priority (MCP tools — use in this order)

| Need | Tool (MCP: `codebase-memory-mcp`) |
|------|-----------------------------------|
| Big-picture overview | `get_architecture` |
| Find symbol by name/label | `search_graph` |
| Find code by meaning | `search_graph` + `semantic_query` |
| Full-text / error message / string literal | `search_code` |
| Call chain / impact | `trace_path` |
| Read specific symbol body | `get_code_snippet` |
| Git diff blast radius | `detect_changes` |
| Complex graph traversal | `query_graph` (Cypher) |
| Check index health | `index_status` |
| Persist design decisions | `manage_adr` |

---

## FORBIDDEN: Grep / Glob / Shell as Primary Search

**You MUST NOT use Cursor `Grep`, `Glob`, broad `Read`, or PowerShell/shell search for code exploration.**
These are **last-resort fallbacks only** — after codebase-memory returns nothing useful.

### Mandatory search routing

| User intent | Use this (NOT Grep/Glob/Shell) |
|-------------|-------------------------------|
| "Where is X defined?" | `search_graph` with `name_pattern` |
| "Find files matching pattern" | `search_graph` with `name_pattern` + `file_pattern` |
| "Who calls X?" | `trace_path` |
| "How does X work?" (by concept) | `search_graph` with `semantic_query` |
| "Search for string/error text" | `search_code` |
| "Show me the code of X" | `get_code_snippet` |
| "Project structure / routes" | `get_architecture` |
| "What does my diff affect?" | `detect_changes` |
| "List files in folder" (for code) | `get_architecture` or `search_graph` |

### Decision gate — before calling Grep, Glob, or Shell search

Ask yourself in order:

1. Can `search_graph` answer this? → **Use it. Stop.**
2. Can `search_code` find the text? → **Use it. Stop.**
3. Can `semantic_query` find it by meaning? → **Use it. Stop.**
4. Do I only need one symbol's body? → **`get_code_snippet`. Stop.**
5. Did all MCP tools return zero relevant results? → Try Cursor `Grep`/`Glob` (state why).
6. Did Grep/Glob also fail? → **Only then** use `rtk grep` / `rtk find` (state why).
7. When using any fallback, **document why** codebase-memory was insufficient.

### Anti-patterns (never do these)

```
❌ Grep / rtk grep for component/function/hook/slice names
❌ Glob / rtk find to locate *.tsx files by naming pattern
❌ PowerShell Get-ChildItem / dir / ls to explore src/ structure
❌ Read 5+ files to "explore" when search_graph could locate symbols
❌ Grep + Read chains as default exploration workflow
❌ Skipping MCP "because grep is faster"
❌ Running shell search before trying search_graph / search_code
❌ Using SemanticSearch or Task explore when MCP is available
```

### Allowed uses of Grep / Glob / Shell (exceptions only)

| Exception | Example |
|-----------|---------|
| Non-indexed file types | `.css`, `.json`, `.md`, config files outside `src/` |
| Exact multiline regex MCP/Grep cannot express | Complex cross-line pattern (rare) |
| File known to exist, reading for edit | `Read` on **one specific file** identified via MCP |
| MCP returned stale/missing after re-index attempt | Document failure, then Grep |
| MCP server unavailable / auth error | Document failure, then fallback |
| User explicitly asks for raw grep/shell | Honor request |

For shell-based search fallback (last resort), use `rtk grep` / `rtk find` — never raw PowerShell grep/find.

---

## When Built-in Tools Are Allowed (Summary)

| Tool | Allowed when |
|------|-------------|
| MCP tools | **Always first** for indexed TypeScript/TSX in `src/` |
| `Read` | **One known file** for editing, path from MCP |
| `Grep` / `Glob` | After MCP fails — document why |
| `rtk grep` / `rtk find` | After MCP **and** Grep/Glob fail — document why |
| Shell (git/npm/build) | Build, test, git — not code search |

`search_code` and `get_code_snippet` always beat `Grep` + `Read` for indexed code.

---

## Index Maintenance

- Graph artifact: `.codebase-memory/graph.db.zst` (commit this for teammates).
- If search returns stale/missing symbols after large refactors or `git pull`, re-index:
  call MCP `index_repository` with `persistence: true`, or ask user: *"Index this project"*.
- After adding many new files, run `detect_changes` to confirm the graph reflects current code.
- Check health: `index_status` for project `D-barber-beauty-saloon-front-V2`.

| **2** | **`rtk`** shell | Git, npm, build, test, lint — operational commands only |
| **3** | `rtk grep` / `rtk find` | **Last resort** — only after MCP **and** Cursor Grep/Glob failed |

content.text:
قوانین Cursor تقویت شد تا **codebase-memory-mcp** همیشه اولویت اول باشد.

## چه تغییری کرد

### `.cursor/rules/codebase-memory.mdc` (`alwaysApply: true`)
- بلوک **CRITICAL / BLOCKING** در ابتدای rule
- **Priority Stack** — MCP → Read → Grep/Glob → Shell
- جدول **MCP Replaces Shell Search** — هر دستور PowerShell/shell با جایگزین MCP
- **Pre-Tool Checklist** — قبل از Grep/Glob/Shell باید از MCP استفاده شود
- Decision gate 7 مرحله‌ای (MCP → Grep → `rtk grep`)

### `.cursor/rules/rtk.mdc`
- Shell فقط برای git/npm/build/test — **نه** جستجوی کد
- `rtk grep` / `rtk find` / `rtk ls` / `rtk read` ممنوع برای exploration وقتی MCP در دسترس است

### `AGENTS.md` §3
- Priority stack و جدول MCP vs Shell
- Decision gate شامل shell search

---

## ترتیب اجباری

```
1. codebase-memory-mcp  (search_graph, search_code, get_architecture, ...)
2. Read                 (فقط یک فایل مشخص — بعد از MCP)
3. Grep / Glob          (فقط اگر MCP جواب نداد + دلیل)
4. rtk grep / rtk find  (آخرین راه — بعد از MCP و Grep)
```

---

**یک بار Cursor را reload کنید** تا rules جدید بارگذاری شوند. اگر بخواهید commit کنم بگویید.

[REDACTED]
